title: cerastium arvense subsp. thomasii
intro: Cerastium arvense subsp. thomasii, commonly known as mountain chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of mountain chickweed include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Mountain chickweed can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a versatile natural remedy for various health concerns.
